description: I have genuinely enjoyed bringing Figma to my team and learning more and more about its capabilities each day! It has been a huge addition to my design team and we are loving our experience!
author: Mai M.
pros: I have become the biggest and newest fan of Figma! I was introduced by a client and now have brought it into my own company and am using it nearly daily!
cons: NO cons! It's an incredible value, incredibly user friendly and honestly just a fun platform to use!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Figma has been a great tool—clean, modern, and easy to work with, especially in a cross-functional setup. It improves clarity between design and development and speeds up reviews, and even with the occasional performance or workflow friction, it’s still one of the most practical design tools I’ve used.
author: Rakhi V.
pros: Figma makes collaboration feel effortless—designs are always up to date, and it’s easy to comment directly on the exact element without back-and-forth. The design system support is also a big win; components, variants, and shared libraries make UI work more consistent and faster to review.
cons: Large files can get heavy, and performance can dip when a project has lots of pages, components, or prototypes. Also, version history and branching are useful, but they can feel a bit confusing unless the team is very disciplined with naming and workflows.

description: Figma does what we need it is great to have our designs in one place to ensure consistency accross the areas we work with , the develeopers of the app we are creating use these designs to work on the front end developement so a very im portant tool for our business
author: Katie B.
pros: We utiise this for Ux designingfor a new app that we are creating , to allow us to collabrate accross the various developement teams to ensure we have a source of truth and we can update and ensure that these have version control
cons: I do find it quite tricky to navigate around and it can be very slow at times and also loses connection

review_title: You'd be crazy to use anything else at this point in time!
description: I'd used multiple different UI/UX design tools as a product manager. There was JustInMind, Sketch, Adobe XD, and a few others, but Figma was a genuine breath of fresh air. I have no plans of switching platforms again, and I know they will continue releasing even more useful features and improvements.
author: Matt B.
pros: From a product management standpoint, it provides us our one-source-of-truth for all of our product designs, and it still gives me the freedom to experiment with designs and make prototypes. It also helps out our developers in a massive way. They have a very clear vision of what the final product will need to look like.
cons: It's difficult to come up with any true downsides to using Figma, but I could mention that you really need to take time to learn the different modes, methods of editing, keyboard shortcuts, and especially how formatting works. I banged my head against the wall several times initially, but its because I attempted to jump right in without doing the homework beforehand.

description: As a QA standpoint, Figma has genuinely improved my testing workflow. Having a single source of truth for design means fewer back-and-forth conversations with the design team. I use it regularly to cross-check UI elements, validate user flows, and identify inconsistencies between the design and the developed product. However, I do wish it had more QA-friendly features like annotation tracking or change diff views. Overall, it's a solid tool that makes cross-functional collaboration much smoother
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like most about Figma is how easy it makes the handoff process between designers and testers. I can directly inspect design elements — spacing, fonts, colours, dimensions — without having to ask the designer every time. The prototyping and flow features are especially useful for me to understand the intended user journey before writing test cases. It gives me a clear reference point to validate the actual product against the expected design. Good in security that without access you can view the design.
cons: My biggest frustration is the lack of proper version history visibility for QA purposes. When a design gets updated mid-sprint, there's no strong notification system to alert testers about what exactly changed. I've missed design changes that directly impacted my test cases because of this. Also, comparing two versions of a design side by side isn't as smooth as it should be, which makes regression checks on UI designs a bit tedious.

description: The overall experience has been really good. It's affordable to get started. The user experience is easy for collaboration and building product prototypes
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Figma is a great design tool that helps him collaborate with both engineering, design, and product teams.
cons: Figma can get overly complicated, and the AI features still need improvement, especially in terms of setup and ease of use.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I have been using Figma for various client projects for about 5 years now. It is my go to for designing and prototyping websites. It makes communicating and collaborating with dev team easy. The software keeps improving over the years and now has better features for vector illustrations as well as faster prototyping with figma make.
cons: The new feature, figma make, is fun to tinker around with. I think it has room for improvement on the generated designs, but it gives an excellent starting point, making my workflow faster.

review_title: Great for collaboration, takes time to learn
description: My overall experience with Figma has been good. It’s a very useful tool for design and teamwork, and once you get used to it, it’s quite straightforward. It really helps with collaboration, although there’s a bit of a learning curve at the start.
author: Portia A.
pros: I like how easy it is to design and collaborate with others in real time. Everything is in one place and it makes sharing ideas and working as a team much smoother
cons: It can be a bit confusing at first, especially if you’re new to design tools. Also, some features are limited unless you upgrade.

description: Overall is has been positive experience and getting better at it as I use it more and more. The usage for free users could be a better to enhance the experience.
author: George F.
pros: Customization and the ability to add other information to enhance the project has been welcomed, also with plugins to other apps that help a lot with integration with projects.
cons: Learning curve is medium and also it tends to have so much happening the you lose yourselve in something posibilities

description: My experience with Figma is good. It is fun and helps me make designs and work with mu team. Customer support helps if there is a problem. It is easy to move around and find tools.
author: Simon S.
pros: I like Figma because it is easy to use. I can make designs and prototypes. My team can work together on the same file. My design are secure.
cons: Sometimes it is slow if the design file is big. Some advances features need the paid version. It can be confusing for beginners at first. The free version has limits on storage and projects.
author_profession: Development and engineering, Design and creative, Internship and entry roles
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A web based tool designed to be web based and surprise it's for the web.
description: I'm happy with Figma and find it replacing certain tasks which I would have previously done with Illustrator. The other very positive thing is 3rd parties can also share templates which you access with Figma and I have noticed that several companies now provide figma based Facebok Ads, Conversion website templates in Figma as it's a bit like Google docs but for images and graphics, with the ability to also edit them and change them.
author: Charles A.
pros: I like that it's web-based so it's easy to share with remote workers who can easily access it.
cons: There's a bit of a learning curve when it comes to auto-fitting for different web formats.

author: Chiara G.
pros: I love that I can create design system, prototype all in one program that it's easy to use, intuitive and user-friendly
cons: I don't like that it's not possible to handle properly vectorial files, therefore Illustrator is needed. it would be cool if the vectorial files could be handled in there

description: I love Figma. I use it every day. There are still some little bugs to be worked out, but it's preferable to a program like Photoshop for designers in many ways.
author: Ryan H.
pros: I love how easy it is to export entire projects. I can make an entire document in figma, order it the way I want it, and export it right then and there as a PDF. I also like how much easier it is to organize than something like Photoshop. I don't have to worry about organizing my desktop files. It's all right there in Figma. It has become my top-used desktop app.
cons: The free version is pretty restrictive. I would like to see more pages and files allowed for people who don't want to pay. It can also be difficult to open shared files. Links automatically open in the browser, and unless you delete your cache and/or cookies, it won't load.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Autolayout becomes easy to manage and creating components and their varients.
cons: Dev mode is not available in free version. and sometimes its laggy.

review_title: Has almost fully replaced Photoshop in my workflow
description: Overall I have been very pleased and have shifted the majority of my design work into the tool
author: James P.
pros: I find the tools very intuitive and for a web design or app design it just works
cons: You have to have all of your graphic assets ready. For example if you are doing more than just basic photo editing, you still need a tool like photoshop.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Overall, Figma has been a huge help for us in developing and testing website and app design ideas. It’s easy to use, keeps the team aligned, and saves a lot of time compared to older design workflows. The collaboration features are the real highlight for me.
author: Chen F.
pros: Figma makes it super easy to collaborate on design concepts with the whole team. I really like how simple it is to test different ideas quickly and share them for instant feedback. Being able to invite teammates and let them comment directly on the designs is a big plus. It keeps everyone involved, makes suggestions easier to follow, and keeps the project moving forward. The desktop version is very stable and stays perfectly in sync with the web and cloud, so I can work anywhere without worrying about losing progress. On top of that, the interface is clean and intuitive, which makes creating concepts and prototypes fast and straightforward.
cons: It can get a bit slow when working with very large design files. Also, if you want to use certain advanced features or export code, you’ll need to be on a paid plan, which adds extra cost.

review_title: Best for pricing, but we can do better
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Very easy to design simple things but also complicate ones. It's very easy how you can share the design with other team members.
cons: Pricing is a bit high and it's a thing I don't like. It would be good to have more features in the free tier.

description: Overall it has been an enjoyable experience which has helped my business growth exponentially
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I love the simplicity and efficiency and options to customize designs to how I want. The collaborative approach also makes it easy to share my designs with my team
cons: It can be quite expensive which makes it difficult to upgrade to higher tiers

review_title: Figma: The new PhotoShop tool in Town
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I love the fact that they have outdone Adobe Photoshop. In the past, when I had to use Adobe, it was a nightmare with overloaded options. I felt like I needed to do a separate course to get a grip on it. But Figma is super simple and minimalistic, making it so user-friendly.
cons: No cons at the moment, Although sometimes its harder to navigate when using sidebars, which is a bummer.

author: Marina F.
pros: I like how intuitive Figma is, and also how it embraces its community.
cons: I haven't found any cons about Figma. There's nothing about Figma that I dislike.

review_title: It is a great platform for user interface design and it is user friendly
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: In the figma prototyping is easy , it's a very user friendly design tool and I love this platform,it also gathers the feedback, It also provides the kits to design the interface, overall experience is good
cons: The icons in figma are limited for free version,they should add the ai made icons for betterment or the modern icons used in other interface

description: The first time I used it was three years ago to design my first website. Without any prior knowledge, I managed to create the design and some simple interactions. Now I am working on my second website and am in the process of learning more so that I can get the most out of it.
author: Josep R.
pros: The perfect software for designing websites and thinking about the user experience, it also allows you to create small creative elements for different formats. The interface is much more attractive and intuitive than other design software.
cons: I think that although it is a intuitive software, you need some kind of training to be able to use it to its full potential.

review_title: Easy to use vector design tool with powerful customizations
description: Really positive, so easy to use, cloud based, fast and reliable.
author: Colin B.
pros: Easy to use vector design tool, it's straightforward to work with different shapes, layers and import images as well as the flexiblity on fonts used.
cons: It's sometimes a little restricting, for example it would be good to have an eraser tool on images so it's easy to have the design output that you desire.

description: Figma , from my experience , meets the need for mockup design , and allows for proper collaboration.
author: Demi M.
pros: It's one of the best online collaboration platforms. The main use is obviously UX, but it also allows several people involved in mockup creation to take part.
cons: Noting to dislike, Figma is affordable for any web interface conception. Ideal for UX and copywriters to better refine their work.

description: My overall experience with Figma has been highly positive. It’s an intuitive, collaborative, especially for projects involving multiple people. The real-time collaboration is the vest feature.
author: Renata C.
pros: the collaboration features and ease of use
cons: the number of files with the free user base and performance issues on large Files
